summary: Joel briefly announces that Fog Creek built a compiler to automatically convert FogBugz's ASP source code to PHP, enabling the product to run on Unix in addition to Windows. Rather than maintaining two separate codebases or doing a manual one-time port, they chose to build an automated translation tool. The post serves as a teaser directing readers to a longer article detailing this technical approach.
key_points: FogBugz needed to support both Unix and Windows platforms
Rather than manually porting ASP to PHP, Fog Creek built an ASP-to-PHP compiler
The automated approach avoids the maintenance burden of two separate codebases
money_quotes: Rather than do a one-time port, we built a compiler that automatically generates a PHP version from the ASP source code.
key_insight: When faced with cross-platform requirements, building an automated source-to-source compiler can be preferable to maintaining separate codebases or doing a manual port.
